text: Entering this space, I feel a strange excitement mixed with curiosity. The glow of neon invites me inward, its artificial blue against the blandness of the hallway stirs something in my imagination. The clarity of rational design here coexists with a faint apprehension—a philosophical nudge toward questioning the nature and consequence of research and development. Inside, the wall asks me to contemplate darkness and possibility. I am aware of my own thoughts swirling with uncertainty, but I also sense the quiet invitation to reflect. It’s as if this place has been staged for philosophical pause.
